varieties.name: Opuntia ficus-indica (Standard Indian Fig) 🏆
varieties.origin: Central Mexico, cultivated since pre-Columbian times (1000+ years)
varieties.traits: Large oval pads 30-60cm long, blue-green color, spineless or few spines; grows 3-5m tall
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, can produce 150-200 fruits per mature plant annually
varieties.availability: Legal in Queensland - available from specialized nurseries in NSW/Victoria that verify legal O. ficus-indica status

varieties.name: Burbank Spineless (Luther Burbank Hybrid) 🏆
varieties.origin: Santa Rosa, California, USA (1907-1925); hybrid of O. ficus-indica × O. robusta
varieties.traits: Large blue-grey pads 40-45cm, completely thornless mature pads; upright growth to 2-3m
varieties.yield_notes: Very high pad production 3-6 pads per growing season; moderate fruit production

varieties.name: Yellow Fruited Spineless (Blanca Type)
varieties.origin: Mexico, selected from wild O. ficus-indica populations
varieties.traits: Large thick pads, minimal to no spines, vigorous upright growth
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy fruit producer, excellent pad production
varieties.availability: Specialty Queensland/NSW nurseries (confirm O. ficus-indica lineage for legal compliance)

varieties.name: Algerian (Muscaredda) 🏆
varieties.origin: Algeria/Mediterranean region, traditional cultivar with centuries of cultivation
varieties.traits: Medium-large pads, white flowers (unusual), spineless or minimal spines
varieties.availability: Rare in Australia; may be imported as O. ficus-indica cultivar from international sources

varieties.name: Sanguigna (Red Fruited)
varieties.traits: Medium pads, red flowers, minimal spines
varieties.availability: Limited in Australia; specialty importers of legal O. ficus-indica varieties

varieties.name: Opuntia ficus-indica (Standard Indian Fig) 🏆
varieties.origin: Central Mexico, introduced to India ~1780, naturalized across Indian subcontinent
varieties.traits: Large oval pads 30-60cm, blue-green, mostly spineless; grows 3-5m tall
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity in well-managed conditions, 5-20 tons fruit per hectare
varieties.availability: Widely available from Indian agricultural nurseries, particularly in Rajasthan, Gujarat, Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: Spineless Cochineal (Nopalea cochenillifera) 🏆
varieties.origin: Mexico/Central America, cultivated for cochineal dye production since Aztec times
varieties.traits: Completely spineless green pads, smaller than O. ficus-indica; grows 1.5-3m
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent pad production for vegetable use, moderate fruit yield
varieties.availability: Available from specialized Indian cactus nurseries, tropical monsoon climates agricultural research stations

varieties.name: Mexican Thornless (Selected Strain)
varieties.origin: Mexico, selected from O. ficus-indica populations for spineless trait
varieties.traits: Large pads, completely thornless, vigorous growth
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity for both pads and fruits
varieties.availability: Available from Indian agricultural research institutes, cactus collections in Rajasthan and Gujarat

varieties.name: Algerian (Muscaredda) 🏆
varieties.origin: Algeria/Mediterranean, introduced to India in colonial period
varieties.availability: Limited in India; may be available through international agricultural exchanges

varieties.name: Yellow Fruited (Blanca)
varieties.origin: Mexico, selected for yellow fruit trait
varieties.traits: Large pads, minimal spines, yellow flowers

varieties.name: Red Fruited (Roja/Sanguigna)
varieties.traits: Medium pads, red/pink flowers, minimal spines

varieties.name: Brazilian Selection (Heat Tolerant)
varieties.origin: Brazil, selected for tropical adaptation and heat tolerance
varieties.traits: Large vigorous pads, spineless, adapted to humid tropics
varieties.availability: May be available through international agricultural cooperation programs

varieties.name: ICARDA Selection (India-Adapted) 🌱
varieties.origin: ICARDA (International Center for Agricultural Research in Dry Areas) selections for Indian conditions
varieties.traits: Selected for Indian climate adaptation, spineless, disease-resistant
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity - documented 30% increase in milk yield per hectare when used as fodder
varieties.availability: ICAR research stations, agricultural universities, state agriculture departments

varieties.name: Traditional Rajasthan Fodder Type 🏆
varieties.origin: Rajasthan, India - cultivated for 200+ years in arid regions
varieties.traits: Hardy large pads, variable spine levels, drought-adapted
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent biomass production in dry conditions
